title: Professional Trolls on YouTube of the future when TV dies

enrichment.summary: Kyle speculates about what happens when TV viewership declines and people migrate to YouTube, predicting that wealthy interests currently funding Fox News will instead distribute money across thousands of YouTube channels as 'professional trolls' to maintain ideological influence. He connects this to observations about the relationship between wealthy elites and the religious Republican base, arguing the rich don't actually share the beliefs they fund but use religion as a control mechanism. He ties this speculation to a personal anecdote about a YouTuber named George Potato who demanded payment before promoting anything, which Kyle sees as evidence of paid YouTube influence operations.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: TV is dying and wealthy interests will redirect media money to YouTube channels
Believes funded troll networks on YouTube are likely or already exist
Rich conservatives don't actually believe in the religion they fund — they use it to control the base
The poorest and least educated Americans are most religious; religion is an easy out for people who 'fuck up'
References them as a real precedent for the kind of funded influence he's describing

entities: Kim Davis (implied — county clerk who denied gay marriage licenses, married four times)
